steps.title: Identify the main claim
steps.content: The passage opens with what people typically think (feathered dinosaurs were winged), then uses “However” to introduce the actual main point: many non-winged dinosaurs also had feathers, exemplified by Yutyrannus.

steps.title: Match the main claim to the purpose
steps.content:
The passage's purpose is to discuss the presence of feathers on different types of dinosaurs—both winged and wingless.

Choice A directly expresses this. The remaining choices introduce topics absent from the text: bird feather differences (B), extinction (C), and a false claim of single-species exclusivity (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
